signals.reasoning: Cover the live SOL short now. It is still green, but it has already given back about 76% of peak unrealized P&L, retained only about 24%, is flagged urgent, still has no stop, and the board is back under veto with a relief-style headline that works against the short thesis.
BTC still shows trend-supported strength and the relief-style headline keeps it from being a clean fresh short. Under veto it stays watch-only.
ETH remains structurally strong and does not offer a cleaner bearish edge than simply closing the weak SOL short. It stays on watch.
GRIFFAIN is already deep into a liquidation-style flush and does not look early enough to chase fresh.
BOME is moving with the relief tape, but the setup quality is too incomplete to justify action during veto.
STABLE has negative-funding squeeze fuel, but price is still weak and open interest says shorts are pressing. It is interesting, but not clean enough to act on while veto is active.
GRASS also looks like a completed liquidation flush rather than an early fresh short. Better to pass than chase.
SUPER has some squeeze-long support from negative funding, but the move is already underway and veto keeps it in watch mode rather than buy mode.
LDO remains a strong continuation name, but it is too extended to chase and too strong to fade casually in this regime.
XPL has upside momentum, but the structure is too incomplete and too stretched to justify fresh risk.
